part_a.text: Define Entropy and state its significance in data compression.
Why Huffman encoding is known as optimal prefix code?
Using RLE encoding techniques generate the compressed sequence for the input aaaaaaaaabbbbbbecececececececececddddddddddddddddecb and justify whether the compression is efficient?
Define the unit decibles (db) and what aspect of the audio signal does it measure?
State the significance of sampling and quantization in video processing.
List any six file formats that can be used to store image contents.
Draw the structure of source data format (packs) of MPEG-1 video standard.
How dictionary encoding differs from Huffman coding?
Describe as of why lossless and lossy compressions are used for compressing text data and audio data respectively?
Distinguish between silence and companding strategies for lossy audio compression.

sub_questions.text: Describe in detail how the performance of a compression algorithm can be measured?
Compare the physical and probability data models used in data compression.

Consider a numerical sequence 9,11,11,11,14,13,15,17,16,17,20,21. Show any Reg No.:_______________ of the modelling and coding aspects that can be used for designing a compression technique for the given sequence.
List any four application areas each for lossless and lossy compression techniques.

Consider a sequence which contains characters with the following frequencies: A – 1, B – 1, C – 1, D – 2, E – 3, F – 5, G – 5 and H – 12. Construct the Huffman tree for the following characters and find the optimal code for each.
Distinguish between RLE and Arithmetic Coding Techniques.

Consider a data sequence which contains characters with the following probabilities: A – 0.4, B – 0.3, C – 0.2 and D – 0.1. It is given that the tags generated for the sequence is 0.516928, if so decode the sequence using the Arithmetic coding technique.
State the steps involved in constructing non-binary Huffman tree and how do the generated code sequence differ from that of a binary Huffman coding?
Outline the procedures used in the JPEG encoding and decoding process.
Compare LZ77 and LZ78 dictionary coding techniques.
Consider the sequence ratatatatbabratbatbabrat. Encode the given sequence using LZW algorithm with the initial dictionary as given below. Give the encoded sequence and final dictionary. Index Entry
State the significance of Discrete Cosine Transform used in JPEG compression.
With a diagram explain the working of a colour CRT monitor.
Outline the different layers of MPEG-1 video stream.
Describe motion compensation in video compression.
State the features of MPEG-4 video compression standards.
Define the terms: (a) Hearing Threshold. (b) Frequency Masking. (c) Critical Bands. (d) Temporal Masking.
List the important features of layer-III of the MPEG audio compression standard.
Compare the layer-I and layer-II of MPEG audio compression standard.
Elaborate the psychoacoustic model used in MPEG audio compression.

part_a.text: Elaborate the relevance of data compression with a few examples
Mathematically define the terms- self information and entropy. What is the significance of entropy in compression?
Explain the term Compression factor in RLE algorithm
List out the characteristics of Huffman code.
Show the working blocks of JPEG LS Algorithm. What are the 2 modes of operation?
Elaborate the concept of dynamic dictionary based coding techniques.
With the support of figures explain the features of progressive and interlaved scanning
Give the characteristics of YUV and YIQ colour models in video.
Mathematically discuss the measurement of sound pressure level (SPL)
Define the terms: (a) Hearing Threshold (b) Critical Bands

sub_questions.text: Elaborate the models for lossless compression with relevant mathematical equations.
Explain the various measures of evaluating the performance of compression algorithms.
With figures and mathematical equations explain the Gaussian model and uniform distribution model for lossy compression. Reg No.:_______________
With an example, elaborate the method of modelling and coding applied for compression of data. Can assume data set as 9,11,11,11,14,13,15,17,16,17,20,21 for the example
With the help of a flowgraph and an example, explain the RLE Compression Algorithm.

A source emits letters from an alphabet A={a1,a2,a3,a4,a5} with probabilities P(a1)= 0.15 ,P(a2)=0.04, P(a3)=0.26,P(a4)=0.05 P(a5)=0.50 (a) Calculate the entropy of this source. (b) Find a Huffman code for this source. (c) Find the average length of the code
Given the probability model below Find the real valued tag for the sequence a1 a1 a3 a2 a3 a1 using Arithmetic coding

A source emits letters from an alphabet A={a1,a2,a3,a4 } with probabilities P(a1)= 0.1 ,P(a2)=0.3, P(a3)=0.25,(a4)=0.35 a. Find the huffman code using 2 methods. b.. Find which of the two codes results in minimum variance.
Consider the sequence : cabracadabrarrarrad . Given window size=13, look ahead buffer size=6, search buffer size=7, Encode the sequence using LZ77 Algorithm.
Give the merits and demerits of LZ77 algorithm
Detail with the help of figures, the steps involved in the JPEG encoding and decoding process
Encode the string ABCDABCABCDA using LZ 78 Algorithm
Describe the concept of motion compensation in video compression. Elaborate the working blocks used in motion compensation with the help of a figure.
Explain the type of frames used in video signal encoding
Illustrate and explain the MPEG1 Video syntax
Give 6 important functionalities of MPEG 4 Video standard
Explain the concept of companding. Bring out the algorithmic approach to approximately obtain the companded code words
Discuss the psychoacoustic model for audio signal processing.
Discuss the unique features of Layer-III compared to other layers in MPEG Audio Coding
With the help of figures explain the concept of Frequency Masking and Temporal Masking in Audio Signal Processing

part_a.text: Discuss different types of compression technique.
Explain distortion in terms of lossy compression techniques
A source emits letters from an alphabet A = [a1, a2, a3, a4, a5] with probabilities P(a1) = 0.15, P(a2) = 0.04, P(a3) = 0.26, P(a4) = 0.05, and P(a5) = 0.50. Find a Huffman code for this source.
Explain prefix code with example.
Compare LZ77 and LZ78.
Discuss the different modes of operation of JPEG Compression.
Explain Motion vector with an example.
Discuss any three application of MPEG-4.
Illustrate the basic MPEG audio coding algorithms.
Explain Digital Audio.

sub_questions.text: Explain probability models for lossy compression

Explain the concepts of Modelling and Coding in Data Compression

A sequence is encoded using the LZ77 algorithm. Given that C(a) = 1, C(b) = 2, C(r) = 3, and C(t) = 4, decode the following sequence of triples: ⟨ 0,0,3⟩, ⟨ 0,0,1⟩, ⟨ 0,0,4 ⟩, ⟨2,8,2⟩, ⟨3,1,2 ⟩, ⟨0,0,3 ⟩, ⟨ 6,4,4⟩, ⟨9,5,4 ⟩ Assume that the size of the window is 20 and the size of the look-ahead buffer is 10.
Encode the decoded sequence in the above question and check if both are same.
